goal: Introduce user notifications through a PWA-first browser channel without third-party Web Push, then add an optional Tauri tray client for users who need background delivery.
global_constraints: Stage 1 must not use Push API, VAPID or third-party browser push endpoints.
Stage 1 must explain that system notifications require an open portal tab or PWA window.
Notification recipients must be resolved server-side.
Notification text must be privacy-safe and short.
Client-side filtering must never be treated as an access-control boundary.
Tauri is optional and must not become required for all users.
Tauri device tokens must be scoped, revocable and stored safely.
Temporary test artifacts and logs belong in .local/.
Update .desc.json and PROJECT_STRUCTURE.yaml after structural changes.
cross_cutting_acceptance: A user can see notifications inside the portal.
A user can enable browser notifications after an explicit action.
A workorder event notifies only users who are allowed to see the workorder.
A user cannot fetch another user's notifications through API.
Browser permission denial leaves the in-portal notification center working.
Closed-browser behavior is documented as server-side backlog only for stage 1.
The stage 2 Tauri design reuses the same server notification queue.
